name: Ocean Eight Vineyard

awards: Mornington Peninsula Wine Show Pinot Trophy

signature: Mike Aylward's precise, minimal cellar door turning out some of the Peninsula's most quietly obsessive pinot noir
editorNote:
Ocean Eight is the kind of cellar door that wine people send other wine people to and casual tasters sometimes overlook. That's a mistake. Mike Aylward grew up in Peninsula wine country — his family founded Kooyong — and Ocean Eight was the project he built when he decided he wanted to do something smaller, quieter, and more single-minded than the big names allow.

The Verve and Aylward Pinot Noirs are among the most structured and ageable wines being made on the Peninsula. The chardonnay holds its own against the region's benchmarks. The tasting room is minimal and unpretentious: you sit down, the wines come out, and Mike or one of the family walks you through them without any of the theatre that creeps into larger cellar doors.

Book ahead — they keep numbers deliberately small. This is a tasting for people who want to understand why the Peninsula matters.

heroImage.alt: Ocean Eight cellar door tasting room with vineyard views on Main Ridge
